modules.name: discord_client
modules.purpose: Discord bot client, event handling, message monitoring

modules.name: message_analyzer
modules.purpose: Detect interesting messages, filter noise, analyze context

modules.name: admin_api
modules.purpose: FastAPI interface for configuration and token management

patterns: Chain of responsibility for message filtering

risks: Discord rate limiting - implement exponential backoff and request queuing
LLM API costs - monitor usage and implement daily spending limits
Message context window overflow - implement smart context truncation
Bot spam detection - add cooldown periods and message frequency limits
Security of admin API - implement proper authentication and HTTPS
Configuration hot-reload race conditions - use proper locking mechanisms
Czech language quality - test LLM responses thoroughly for natural Czech
Fallback chain failures - ensure graceful degradation when all LLMs fail
summary: The architecture follows a modular design with clear separation of concerns. The discord_client module handles Discord integration and events. The message_analyzer implements filtering logic using a chain of responsibility pattern to detect interesting messages. The llm_service provides multi-LLM support with a strategy pattern and circuit breaker for automatic fallback (Claude → Gemini → OpenAI). Configuration is centralized in config_manager with validation and hot-reload support. The admin_api provides a future FastAPI interface for runtime configuration. The storage module handles conversation context persistence. All modules use dependency injection for loose coupling. The system is designed to be containerized with Docker, with separate containers for the Discord bot and admin API if needed. Configuration uses YAML with JSON schema validation, and secrets are managed via environment variables.
